countries.policyOrientation: Expansionary fiscal + protectionist trade. Near century-high tariffs on China.
countries.centralBank: Fed Chair Jerome Powell - inflation-first, defying Trump pressure
countries.keyRisk: Trump-Powell conflict, tariff escalation, mid-term elections 2026
countries.rationale: High volatility from policy uncertainty but US innovation strength remains

countries.policyOrientation: GDP 5%+ target, massive fiscal spending, New Quality Productivity push
countries.centralBank: PBOC Governor Pan Gongsheng - flexible RRR and rate cuts
countries.monetaryDirection: Easing: rate cuts + RRR cuts planned
countries.keyRisk: Property debt crisis, deflation, US tariff war, population decline
countries.rationale: Structural problems (property, demographics) outweigh stimulus efforts

countries.leader: Lee Jae Myung (since June 2025, post-Yoon impeachment)
countries.policyOrientation: Energy security, supplementary budget, economic revitalization. 67% approval rating.
countries.centralBank: New BOK Governor Shin Hyun Song (BIS economist, appointed 2026-03-22) - balanced policy
countries.monetaryDirection: Likely hold for 2026, possible cut if economy deteriorates
countries.keyRisk: Post-impeachment political residual, China export dependency, KRW weakness
countries.rationale: Political stability recovering, semiconductor sector strong, high presidential approval

countries.policyOrientation: Structural reform, wage growth promotion, digital transformation
countries.centralBank: BOJ Governor Ueda Kazuo - rate normalization underway
countries.monetaryDirection: Rate hike cycle, ending ultra-low rate era. January minutes show consensus for more hikes.
countries.rationale: Rate normalization positive for financials, gradual economic improvement

countries.policyOrientation: GDP 7%+ growth, Make in India, digital economy expansion
countries.centralBank: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra - inflation management + growth support
countries.rationale: Highest growth potential, China+1 beneficiary, young demographics, pro-market reforms

countries.policyOrientation: Manufacturing hub development, open reform continuation
countries.centralBank: SBV - 4.5% inflation target, ~15% credit growth target
countries.monetaryDirection: Macro stability priority, gradual easing possible
countries.keyRisk: Leadership succession uncertainty, China economic dependency
countries.rationale: China+1 manufacturing FDI beneficiary, growing middle class

countries.policyOrientation: Large infrastructure spending, food self-sufficiency, expanded health insurance
countries.centralBank: BI Governor Perry Warjiyo - inflation targeting, but independence concerns
countries.keyRisk: Policy unpredictability, central bank independence under threat, Prabowo nephew nominated as BI deputy
countries.rationale: Political intervention in central bank, populist policies, unpredictable direction
